title: Bases pour servir aux entreprises de colonisation  dans les territoires nationaux de la Republique Argentine
summaries: "Bases pour servir aux entreprises de colonisation dans les territoires nationaux de la République Argentine" by Docteur Auguste Brougnes is an economic and agricultural treatise written in the late 19th century. The work discusses the principles of colonization as a means to alleviate agricultural poverty in Europe, while promoting the fertile lands of Argentina as an investment opportunity for struggling European farmers.   The opening of the work introduces the author's intentions and motivations for developing a comprehensive colonization plan aimed at addressing the economic hardships faced by small farmers in Europe. Brougnes reflects on his observations during journeys through Argentina, noting its growth in agriculture and the influx of European colonists drawn by the promise of a better life. He emphasizes the necessity of a coordinated effort involving land, labor, and capital as the foundation for successful colonization initiatives, aiming to empower impoverished European farmers with the means to cultivate thriving lives in the fertile Argentine territories.
